name: Coramitug reduced NT-proBNP but not 6-minute-walk distance
summary: Coramitug supplies recent phase 2 direct-clearance biomarker evidence in ATTR-CM, while blocking a functional-benefit upgrade for this depleter-antibody class.
statement: In ATTR cardiomyopathy, 60 mg/kg coramitug significantly reduced NT-proBNP compared with placebo at week 52, but neither dose significantly improved 6-minute-walk distance.
population_or_model: Patients with ATTR cardiomyopathy, most receiving background disease-modifying therapy
time_horizon: 52-week treatment period with safety follow-up to 64 weeks
quantitative_note: The 60 mg/kg dose reduced NT-proBNP by 48% versus placebo (95% CI -65% to -22%; P=0.0017); 6-minute-walk change was not statistically different from placebo.
caveats: The biomarker result is promising but does not yet prove functional recovery or outcome benefit.
Most participants were already receiving disease-modifying ATTR therapy, complicating attribution.
The sample was modest, mostly male, and largely New York Heart Association class II.
